qualifying_org_definition: 501(c)(3) or State of Kansas not-for-profit; in-kind/unpaid employment (volunteering) explicitly counts per KEESM 2520; work can be 'for pay, for goods or services, unpaid, or as a volunteer' per DCF guidance
verification_instrument: Signed letter or statement from a supervisor or nonprofit organization documenting volunteer hours (per DCF guidance cited in KEESM 1322.1(8)); timesheets also accepted; no named Kansas-specific form number
hours_formula_detail: KEESM 2520 states '20 hours per week (averaged monthly)' — equivalent to the federal 80 hours/month standard under 7 CFR 273.24; no workfare allotment formula used
accepts_outofstate_detail: KEESM 2520, KEESM 1322, and DCF guidance cite 'a nonprofit organization' or '501(c)(3)' without any in-state location requirement. No Kansas policy or regulation restricts the certifying organization to a Kansas-based entity. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) imposes no such restriction either. Operationally, a CA-based 501(c)(3) signing verification letters should be accepted.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Kansas did not hold any statewide or county-level ABAWD waivers prior to the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (2025), and no Kansas waivers appear in the FNA FY2025-2029 waiver listings. All 105 Kansas counties are subject to the ABAWD work requirement.
submission_deadline: Kansas uses a change-reporting model: recipients must report when work hours drop below 20/week 'soon after the change becomes known.' Positive verification of hours meeting the requirement is typically presented at recertification or when requested by the case worker, not on a fixed monthly schedule. No specific day-of-month deadline found.
notes:
KEESM 2520 explicitly permits the ABAWD work requirement to be met via 'in-kind work and unpaid employment (volunteering)' at 20+ hrs/week with no county assignment, Employment Development Plan, or MOU required — this is a clean general volunteer lane separate from the formal E&T program (KEESM 3310), which does require cooperative agreements (ES-4104/ES-4105) and county work-site assignment. Verification of hours is mandatory per KEESM 1322.1(8) but no specific Kansas form number is named for the volunteer lane; in practice a signed timesheet from the org appears to suffice. Confidence is medium because no named state verification form was found and the HOPE Act FAQ PDF was not parseable, leaving the exact documentation instrument slightly uncertain.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial adversarial review to attempt to refute the Tier 1 classification. Searched for: (1) work registration / intake prestep (E-26), (2) SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer track requirements, (3) approved volunteer site lists or nonprofit MOU requirements, (4) county-level variation, (5) anti-hunger legal aid explainers. Key findings: (a) VERIFICATION INSTRUMENT RESOLVED — DCF guidance (confirmed via KEESM 3100 search results) explicitly states volunteer hours may be verified by 'a signed letter from a supervisor or non-profit organization' or timesheets; no named Kansas form number exists for this purpose. (b) E-26 WORK REGISTRATION is required for all non-exempt SNAP applicants at the time of application — but this is application-intake paperwork completed when the recipient first enrolls in SNAP, not a separate gating step that must occur before volunteer hours begin counting. It does not require case manager assignment or approval of the volunteer site. (c) NO APPROVED SITE LIST found — no evidence of any pre-approved nonprofit registry or MOU requirement for the general volunteer lane under KEESM 2520. The ES-4104/ES-4105 cooperative agreements apply only to formal E&T assigned worksites (KEESM 3310), not the general volunteer pathway. (d) COUNTY VARIATION: The E&T program operates only in four designated counties (Shawnee, Sedgwick, Johnson, Wyandotte), but the general volunteer lane under KEESM 2520 operates statewide without county restriction. county_variation_flag remains false. (e) FY2026 E&T STATE PLAN PDF is binary-encrypted and could not be parsed directly, but search metadata confirms it is titled 'State Plan KS FY26' and available at dcf.ks.gov. (f) 2025 federal SNAP changes (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) raised ABAWD age ceiling to 64, expanding the population subject to the requirement in Kansas, but did not alter the volunteer pathway mechanism. (g) No evidence found of any EDP requirement, case manager assignment, or state-level gating before volunteer hours count. Tier 1 classification SURVIVES adversarial review. Confidence raised to HIGH: the verification instrument is now resolved (signed nonprofit letter/timesheet), no prestep beyond SNAP application exists, no approved site list, and multiple KEESM sections and DCF operational guidance converge on the same picture.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA — KEESM 2520 states '20 hours per week averaged monthly' = 80 hrs/month; Kansas follows the federal_80 formula, not a workfare allotment formula. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT — KEESM and DCF guidance are silent on nonprofit location; no in-state restriction found in any Kansas policy source; federal default applies (any qualifying nonprofit); classified 'silent' with operational expectation of acceptance. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS — Kansas has no statewide or county-level ABAWD waivers; fully enforced across all 105 counties; OBBBA did not affect Kansas since it had no prior waivers. FNA FY2025-2029 waiver page confirmed (direct fetch timed out but multiple search results and prior research corroborate). (4) SUBMISSION UX — DCF change-reporting page confirms hours can be reported via online portal (DCF Self-Service), phone, mail, or fax to local DCF office; Kansas uses a change-reporting model (report when hours drop, not monthly proactive submission); verification letters are presented on request or at recertification. Reporting_cadence set to 'at_recertification' as the primary trigger; no fixed monthly deadline found. (5) FORM FIELDS — No state-specific form exists; the baseline set (org_name, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date) applies; KEESM 1322.1(8) mandates documentation of hours but does not enumerate specific fields beyond establishing that the hours and work nature be documented.
